programme.title: My Cat from Hell

programme.desc: Dove hunting season begins in Texas, while in Beaumont, road hunters get an early start on deer season during a decoy sting

programme.title: Richard E Grants Seven Deadly Sins
programme.desc: Actor Richard E Grant explores the origins of the seven cardinal sins, examining the dramatic effect they have. He begins with the evolutionary roots of wrath, learning that, like humans, animals also experience the sensation. Cutting-edge wildlife footage is used to illustrate his points as wrathful hippopotamuses take centre stage

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: An emaciated horse gives birth prematurely, and the foal is so weak Dr Thurston takes it home to bottle feed

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: The animal protection officers have cause to investigate a breeder

programme.title: Boys from the Blackstuff
programme.desc: Chrissie and his wife Angie are driven to despair over their financial problems, to say nothing of their constant hounding from the Department of Employment investigators. Starring Michael Angelis, Julie Walters and Jean Boht

programme.title: Tribe
programme.desc: Bruce Parry treks into the jungles of Sarawak, Borneo, in search of the last nomadic Penan, a tribe of hunter-gatherers whose forest home is in the process of being cut down around them. Their traditional way of life is about to disappear

programme.title: Canals: The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor tells the story of the early canal builders who struggled with the rugged terrain of Englands Pennine hills. Creating a network of canals in this landscape was an major challenge, but connecting the powerhouses of Yorkshire and Lancashire was a great incentive at the time of the Industrial Revolution. Liz considers the strategic considerations and implications of this massive engineering project

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.sub-title: Weatherman Walking: Dylans Walks - New Quay and Laugharne
programme.desc: Derek Brockway presents the second of two specials originally shown to celebrate the centenary of Dylan Thomass birth. First he travels to the seaside town of New Quay on Cardigan Bay, where the poet and writer lived with wife Caitlin during the Second World War. Jeff Towns, chairman of the Dylan Thomas Society, joins Derek to explain how Thomas was inspired to write some of his greatest work during this era. For his final walk, to Laugharne, the presenter is accompanied by Thomass grandaughter Hannah Ellis, who reveals the significance of the town to both her grandparents and her family today

programme.title: Canals: The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor recounts the story of canal mania - a boom period of frenzied activity that helped develop Britains modern financial economy, now centred in London. The new canal capitalists made money by investing and speculating in waterways used to carry fuel and goods around the country. However, not all canals were profitable and investors soon discovered that such a venture created losers as well as winners

programme.title: Canal Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter embarks on an eight-mile trip along the Caledonian Canal, the waterway that cuts through beautiful Scottish mountain country and is regarded as one of the most ambitious canals of its time. Its engineer, Thomas Telford, intended not only to create an engineering marvel, but also provide badly needed jobs and wealth for the Highlands

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett travels from Britain to Jerusalem via Sicily, examining the expansion and unchecked ambition of the Norman empire, and how its impact still resonates in todays culture and politics. He discovers how, under the leadership of Scandinavian raider Rollo, these descendants of the Vikings first established themselves in northern France and became an unstoppable force of Christian knights and warriors

programme.title: Misery (1990)
programme.desc: A successful author finds his relief at being rescued from a near-fatal car crash is severely tempered by the discovery that his saviour is a fan with psychopathic tendencies. The situation takes a turn for the worse when she discovers he has killed off her favourite character - and sets about coercing him to do a rewrite in nightmarish fashion. Oscar-winning thriller, starring James Caan, Kathy Bates and Lauren Bacall

programme.title: Mark Lawson Talks To
programme.sub-title: Mark Lawson Talks to Stephen King
programme.desc: Interview with renowned author Stephen King, known for influential horror novels such as Carrie, The Shining and The Tommyknockers. His other popular works outside the genre include The Shawshank Redemption, The Green Mile and Stand By Me

programme.title: Light and Dark
programme.desc: The first of a two-part documentary in which Professor Jim Al-Khalili examines how some of historys greatest minds began to uncover the secrets of the universe by using and manipulating light. In the first edition, the presenter considers the significance of Greek mathematician Euclids landmark discovery that light moves in straight lines. He then moves on to Renaissance Italy, where Galileo Galilei unlocked the potential of telescopes in locating Earths true place within the cosmos, before assessing the implications of Danish astronomer Ole Roemers groundbreaking finding - that light moves at a finite speed

programme.title: Romancing the Stone: The Golden Ages of British Sculpture
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke examines sculpture in the 20th century, an era of innovation, scandal and creativity. He examines the artistic revolution spearheaded by Eric Gill and Jacob Epstein, which paved the way for the work of Gilbert and George and Damien Hirst. He also explores the art forms rise in popularity, from First World War memorials to the landmark work of Antony Gormley and Rachel Whiteread

programme.title: Canals: The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor recounts the story of canal mania - a boom period of frenzied activity that helped develop Britains modern financial economy, now centred in London. The new canal capitalists made money by investing and speculating in waterways used to carry fuel and goods around the country. However, not all canals were profitable and investors soon discovered that such a venture created losers as well as winners

programme.title: Canal Walks with Julia Bradbury

Its engineer, Thomas Telford, intended not only to create an engineering marvel, but also provide badly needed jobs and wealth for the Highlands

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett travels from Britain to Jerusalem via Sicily, examining the expansion and unchecked ambition of the Norman empire, and how its impact still resonates in todays culture and politics. He discovers how, under the leadership of Scandinavian raider Rollo, these descendants of the Vikings first established themselves in northern France and became an unstoppable force of Christian knights and warriors

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jayne Middlemiss presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on May 14, 1999. Featuring 911, Bryan Adams, Whitney Houston, Fierce, Kula Shaker, Pete Heller, Stereophonics and Backstreet Boys

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Scott Mills presents the pop chart programme, first broacast on May 21, 1999. Featuring Shania Twain, Geri Halliwell, Super Furry Animals, Skunk Anansie, Powerhouse featuring Duane Harden, 1000 Clowns, Happy Mondays and Boyzone

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Noel Edmonds presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on March 2, 1978. Featuring performances by Darts, Kate Bush, Nick Lowe, Andy Williams, Samantha Sang, Rita Coolidge, the Tom Robinson Band and Abba

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jakki Brambles presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on February 28, 1991. Featuring performances by N-Joi, Massive Attack, Jesus Jones, Free, the Clash, the Source featuring Candi Staton, Madonna and the Simpsons

programme.title: Simon and Garfunkel: The Concert in Central Park
programme.desc: The duos 1981 reunion concert in New York City, performing favourites including The Sound of Silence, The Boxer, Mrs Robinson, Homeward Bound and Bridge Over Troubled Water, as well as songs from Simons solo career

programme.title: Simon and Garfunkel: The Harmony Game
programme.desc: The story of Simon and Garfunkels 1970 album Bridge Over Troubled Water, which was made during a particularly creative period in the duos career, but turned out to be their final studio LP. The film covers all aspects of the records creation, from technical breakthroughs to the emotional feelings the artists had for each other, and features interviews with Paul Simon, Art Garfunkel and their collaborators, including manager Mort Lewis and drummer Hal Blaine

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: A look through the BBC archives, featuring folk performances by artists including Pete Seeger, Tim Buckley, Joan Baez, Bob Dylan and Leonard Cohen. First broadcast 26 October 1991

programme.title: Peter, Paul and Mary: Rhythm on Two
programme.desc: Part one. A concert given by the well known American folk trio during their 1980s visit to the United Kingdom, recorded on location at the Southport Theatre. First broadcast 28 September 1983

programme.title: Peter, Paul and Mary: Rhythm on Two
programme.desc: The second part of the concert performed by the American folk trio, recorded at the Southport Theatre. First broadcast 26 November 1983

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jayne Middlemiss presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on May 14, 1999. Featuring 911, Bryan Adams, Whitney Houston, Fierce, Kula Shaker, Pete Heller, Stereophonics and Backstreet Boys

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by David Morrissey, Holly Willoughby, John Kearns and Montell Douglas to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Gethin Jones and Holly Hamilton present the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Crimewatch Live

James meets American rally driver Ken Block, and Jeremy tests out the Mercedes SL Black. Comedian Michael McIntyre is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Shooting Joe Exotic
programme.desc: Louis revisits his 2011 documentary Americas Most Dangerous Pets, returning to Oklahoma to explore what has happened since. Joe Exotic, aka Joseph Allen Maldonado-Passage, now resides in a federal prison after being found guilty of a murder-for-hire plot and multiple animal cruelty charges, and having been the subject of hit Netflix documentary Tiger King

programme.title: Green Book (2018)
programme.desc: In the 1960s, working-class Italian-American bouncer Tony Lip becomes the driver of Dr Donald Shirley, an African-American classical pianist on a tour of venues through the American South. Director Peter Farrellys Bafta and Oscar-winning comedy drama, starring Viggo Mortensen, Mahershala Ali and Linda Cardellini

programme.title: Spider-Man 2 (2004)
programme.desc: Peter Parker continues his fight for justice, but unrequited love and personal worries take their toll and he is tempted to abandon his web-slinging alter ego. However, the arrival of deadly new bad guy Doctor Octopus plunges him into a race against time to save New York. Comic-book adventure sequel, with Tobey Maguire, Kirsten Dunst, Alfred Molina, James Franco and Rosemary Harris

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Forbidden America
programme.desc: The film-maker returns to the US to explore the impact of the internet and social media on some of the most controversial corners of American society. He begins by meeting the new influencers of the far-right who are promoting an ideology that is defiantly racist, misogynistic, homophobic and anti-Semitic, often packaging the most extreme views as irony. Louis attempts to understand their recent surge in popularity and challenges the divisive rhetoric that is being streamed to millions of young viewers online

programme.title: How to Win the Champions League
programme.desc: The first instalment of a three-part look at what it takes to achieve continental success, beginning with Real Madrid becoming European champions for a record-extending 15th time. Former players Clarence Seedorf and Sami Khedira reflect on the clubs evolution, the never-say-die Los Blancos mindset, and the decisive factors that led to a sixth UEFA Champions League triumph in the space of a decade

programme.title: How to Win the Champions League
programme.desc: A look at Liverpools 2019 UEFA Champions League triumph, the sixth time they had become champions of Europe. Key players Andy Robertson and Alisson Becker look back at how they narrowly progressed from a tough group, former director of research Ian Graham discusses the recruitment approach that saw them sign players such as Mo Salah, and journalists Barney Ronay and Melissa Reddy recall the memorable semi-final comeback against Barcelona

programme.title: How to Win the Champions League
programme.desc: A profile of Jose Mourinho, who won the UEFA Champions League with FC Porto and Inter Milan

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Documentary following boxer Tommy Furys return to the ring. Tommy is at his lowest point personally and professionally, living alone in a house that was meant to be his and partner Molly-Maes forever home. His role as a father to baby Bambi brings both joy and pressure as he tries to convince Molly-Mae he is a changed man. After finally getting the medical all-clear to fight again, Tommy and his trainer and father John prepare for his return to the ring against MMA fighter Darren Till. Team Fury travels to the press conference - with John promising to be on his best behaviour

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy prepares for his first gym appearance since announcing his comeback, but with fighter Darren Till throwing threats and chaos into the mix, trainer John pulls Tommy out of the fight claiming he is unwilling to risk his sons future career. Its a crushing blow for Tommy, whos desperate for momentum and purpose. Amid the pressure, theres a flicker of hope - Tommy and partner Molly-Mae Hague are spotted together on New Years Eve, with a kiss that hints at a brighter future

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Forbidden America
programme.desc: The film-maker returns to the US to explore the impact of the internet and social media on some of the most controversial corners of American society. He begins by meeting the new influencers of the far-right who are promoting an ideology that is defiantly racist, misogynistic, homophobic and anti-Semitic, often packaging the most extreme views as irony. Louis attempts to understand their recent surge in popularity and challenges the divisive rhetoric that is being streamed to millions of young viewers online

programme.desc: Tommy prepares for his first gym appearance since announcing his comeback, but with fighter Darren Till throwing threats and chaos into the mix, trainer John pulls Tommy out of the fight claiming he is unwilling to risk his sons future career. Its a crushing blow for Tommy, whos desperate for momentum and purpose. Amid the pressure, theres a flicker of hope - Tommy and partner Molly-Mae Hague are spotted together on New Years Eve, with a kiss that hints at a brighter future

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommys fight for redemption continues, but this time, its a battle for his own mind. Struggling to secure a new opponent, he knows a comeback fight is crucial for his mental health. Meanwhile, Molly-Maes new series trailer drops, pushing Tommy to face his own truths in the toughest interview of his life, where he opens up about his battle with alcohol addiction. Back in training, hope returns as a potential fight looms, leading him and his father John to a career-defining meeting

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy is hopeful that he could be fighting again soon, and he and Molly-Mae are spending more time together. Then, disaster strikes when KSI cancels the night when Roman was due to be in the fighting, claiming illness. John is furious, but decides the boys need cheering up so tells them he is taking them on a camping trip. However, neither Tommy nor Roman share their fathers love of the outdoors

programme.title: Escaping Utopia
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on what life was like for the second and third-generation members of Gloriavale, growing up in a world where every decision is made for them. New Zealand journalist Melanie Reid recounts her experience of going undercover inside the community in the 1990s, and the work she did to try to stop the abuse she discovered

programme.title: Escaping Utopia
programme.desc: The drama documentary charts the impact of the loss of Gloriavales charismatic leader Hopeful Christian and his successors struggle to keep the community running. Meanwhile, an underground network helps one member leave, while two former children of Gloriavale expose one of its most shocking secrets

programme.title: The Walsh Sisters
programme.desc: Claire tries to charm a doctor into sharing details, while Anna fixates on whether it is possible to turn right on Harcourt Street. Maggie arranges a place for Rachel at Cloisters, and although she is at first reluctant, she eventually agrees. As Anna waits for updates, the weight of uncertainty begins to settle in and she struggles to find the strength to face what lies ahead

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs compete for Scotland with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by the British film industry. The dishes are judged by Michelin-starred chef Adam Handling, and include a leek and potato veloute inspired by Trainspotting and an aubergine baba ganoush in honour of Limbo. Presented by Andi Oliver

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: A couple whose marriage was pushed to the brink when the menopause altered the wifes attitude to her relationship try to reconnect through a problem-solving challenge. Bear tasks them with keeping an ember alight while gathering fuel for a signal fire, while relationship therapist Simone Bose talks to them about the hurt theyve both experienced

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Compilation episode following comedians Jennifer Saunders, Darren Harriott and Phill Jupitus as they trade punchlines for profits in their for antique treasures. Pointing them in the right direction are experts Philip Serrell, Ishy Khan and James Braxton

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is in Devon as she helps three generations escape from the West Midlands to the West Country - whether they end up all under one roof or side by side. Tasked with the viewings are Matt and his father-in-law Mark, who will then report back to their wives. Away from the search, Nicki also textile designer who swapped city life for rural creativity and even has a go at weaving on a handloom herself

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Monty Don looks back at some of the gardeners shining a light on how people can grow their own nutritious food fresh from the garden. Sue Kent heads to the Welsh borders to meet a grower who turns beautiful blooms into inspired works of art, and Rekha Mistry serves up expert tips from her kitchen garden in the Peak District

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: The Vintage French Farmhouse
programme.desc: Antique dealers Ian Humphries, Harry Khwaja and Jo Roberts go on a buying trip to Sommières in the South of France, looking for items to sell online for a profit. Harry haggles hard on an oversized jewellery box he thinks his American clients will fall in love with, and romance is in the air for Ian too, who takes home a large plaster cherub statue. Jo goes for a hand-built toy washstand she plans on reselling as a decorative piece to her dedicated followers

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson visits the Norfolk village of Cley next the Sea and finds that due to Dutch settlers in the Elizabethan era, its not as next the sea as it once was. Ben talks to historian Onyeka Nubia, who reveals that the village was part of a cosmopolitan community that introduced Britain to the canary and even the beer people drink today

programme.title: Italys Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Using 3D scanning technology, Alexander Armstrong and historian Michael Scott uncover the hidden history of three Italian cities, beginning with Naples, from its earliest Greek and Roman origins. The pair explore the impact of the volcano of Mount Vesuvius, and how it both nurtured the region and exacted a terrible price on the local population. Alexander and Michael also delve into a labyrinth of underground spaces constructed by ruling Bourbon kings in the 19th century, that helped to sustain the city

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Kate Bateman and Adam Partridge value items in the spa town of Malvern, Worcestershire, including a wooden top toy and old coins that could be worth thousands of pounds. Presenter Paul Martin meets a local man who has been making violins for 40 years and tries his hand at woodwork

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Former players Josh Jones, Sarah Keyworth, Melvin Odoom and Kimberly Wyatt test their general knowledge skills ahead of double points Friday

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the penultimate day of the weeks quiz, and todays destination is Bristol. Paul Gorton leads the unreliable tour, dishing out facts and fibs like theyre going out of fashion. Former EastEnders star Nitin Ganatra, the Rev Kate Bottley and Alex Gray, aka Apollo from Gladiators, all want to top the weekly leaderboard

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from Scotland must go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again. They need to impress a panel of exacting judges - chef Tom Kerridge, former Great British Menu Champion of Champions Lorna McNee, and comedian Phil Wang. The guest judge is Bafta-winning actress Katherine Parkinson. How will she judge dishes celebrating the British film industry? Only one of the chefs will triumph and go through to represent Scotland at the national finals. Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: On the Isle of Lewis, Allys pride is at stake as he and son Noah prepare their ewes to take on mainland breeders at Dingwall show and market. The Glenns are hoping for good scanning results after a devastating loss of sheep in winter floods, while the pressure mounts as Lucy and Adam must negotiate an earlier completion date on the farm in Scotland, secure their finances and ensure the dairy herd is disease free

programme.title: Murder Case
programme.sub-title: The Hunt for Arlene Frasers Killer - Part Two
programme.desc: The concluding part charts a decades-long quest for justice, as Arlenes family and the police fight through courtrooms and appeals to uncover the truth about her disappearance. The police investigation is rigorously tested through two murder trials and a dramatic appeals process, with the process going from the High Court in Edinburgh in 2003 to the Scottish Parliament in 2025, where Arlenes sister Carol continued her determined campaign to uncover the truth about what happened

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: As Alan Sugar continues his search for a new business partner, Angela Scanlon and her guests take a deep dive into the candidates performances and analyse the Egypt task, the drama of the boardroom. Plus, a chat with the latest person to leave the process

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the days events

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.sub-title: Derry Girls: Decades of Domestic Abuse
programme.desc: Figures show that on average one in four women living in Northern Ireland will experience domestic abuse in their lifetime. In 1976, two women armed with a bucket and a ladder broke into a building in Londonderry and opened the citys first refuge for women and children fleeing abuse. Five decades on, Foyle Womens Aid remains an ongoing place of safety. Jennifer OLeary uncovers some of their stories as Spotlight investigates a service shaped by the violence of others

programme.title: AI Confidential with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: In March 2018, a driverless car struck and killed a woman crossing the street in Tempe, Arizona, making it the worlds first pedestrian fatality from a car that was being driven by AI. But there was also a human operator behind the wheel, monitoring the vehicle, and a decision had to be made who was responsible, the human, or the AI? In her first TV interview, operator Rafaella Vasquez tells the story of the crash and its aftermath, while Hannah also talks to the lead detective on the case. She also tracks down a whistleblower from Uber, the company behind the driverless vehicle, and takes her first rides in autonomous vehicles in the US and UK

programme.title: LS Lowry: The Unheard Tapes
programme.desc: An insight into the secretive matchstick men painter as he guides viewers through his revealing life story in unheard interviews from the end of his life. Recorded by a young fan, Angela Barratt, in 1972, the tapes uncover much about Lowrys life story and the changing north he captured in his iconic paintings. Lip-synced by actors Ian McKellen and Annabel Smith.

programme.title: Gladiators
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney Walsh host the second quarter-final. Four contenders must face five brutal events before going head to head in the legendary Eliminator. Giant climbs back onto Everest, Sabre defends her territory on The Edge, and Apollo launches a rocket in the Gauntlet

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Scottish chefs serve up their mains and desserts inspired by the British film industry, including venison loin smoked in gunpowder tea inspired by Skyfall and a frangipane custard tart in honour of Brave. Veteran judge Adam Handling helps to decide who deserves to say and who should go home. Presented by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: Norberto Paredes reports from Venezuela on the future of its oil sector, asking whether the return of US firms is the answer the country needs. BBC Eyes forensic investigators also uncover the story of last years mass protests in Nepal that toppled the government. With eyewitness accounts, they reveal where the order to use live ammunition came from and how unarmed demonstrators were killed as authorities lost control of the crowds

programme.title: Clive Myries African Adventure
programme.desc: In Abeokuta, Clive tests his basketball skills on the court with an inspirational coach, before attending the birthday celebrations of a local Gospel singer to see just how Nigerians throw a party. Hell also get hands on to create authentic tie-and-dye Adire clothes and join in with an incredible contemporary dance troupe

programme.title: Clive Myries African Adventure
programme.desc: In Morocco, Clive travels to Marrakesh, known around the world as the Red City for the red clay buildings that line its streets. He meets a Medina chef whose slow roasted lamb, cooked in underground ovens, has had the Royals of approval. In a souk, he masters the art of creating stunning tile mosaics, learns the skills required to make authentic Moroccan rugs, before spending the night in the High Atlas Mountains

programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts a game show in which players compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: The Vintage French Farmhouse
programme.desc: Antiques dealers Jon Swanston, Melissa Drabble and Lucy Ryder Richardson head to the French town of LIsle-sur-la-Sorgue, where there are over 300 stalls and second-hand shops scattered along its picturesque roads and canal banks. Their hope is to pick up two hidden treasures each, to buy and sell for a profit online. Mel strikes a deal on a cast iron umbrella stand, a mysterious pair of metal boots catch Jons eye, while Lucy selects a holy water canteen from Lourdes

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson uncovers the secrets of Cornwalls Tintagel, famed as the legendary birthplace of King Arthur. Ben discovers Tintagel was potentially more important than London a thousand years ago, based on recent evidence unearthed at an archaeological dig overseen by English Heritage. It was found that over 2,000 people were living here which, at the time, which would have made it the largest settlement anywhere of its kind in Britain

programme.title: Italys Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Dr Michael Scott explore the Italian city of Venice, using 3D technology to discover how a city built in a swamp came to be one of the most powerful locations in Medieval Europe. The pair dive deep into the many canals in this unique place to experience how its buildings still remain standing to this day, and highlight how Venices beauty once masked a ruthless secret state, and a world of excess and vice

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team heads to Dulwich College in south-east London, where Michael Baggott falls in love with a Chinese censer, and Kate Bateman casts her eye over a collection of bronze animals. Paul Martin visits the Dulwich Picture Gallery

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Former players Josh Jones, Sarah Keyworth, Melvin Odoom and Kimberly Wyatt test their general knowledge skills before the days winner, and weeks victor is revealed

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the final day of the weeks whistlestop tour of the UK. Former EastEnders star Nitin Ganatra, Rev Kate Bottley and Alex Gray, aka Apollo from Gladiators, are this weeks players, and this is their final chance to claim glory. Paul Gorton is the unreliable tour guide, and hes taking the players to Wrexham

programme.title: The Wild Gardener
programme.desc: Part two of two. During the second year, Colin Stafford-Johnson begins to reap the rewards of his labours. Working through the winter, the wildlife cameraman reflects on his journey so far and plans the busy spring and summer months ahead. With most of the heavy lifting done, Colin will now use a gentle hand to maintain the garden as he tries to lure back as many wild creatures as possible

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Monty Don breathes new life into a tired agapanthus, pots up a fig tree for future harvests and shares a tip for growing roses. A retired GP shows Frances Tophill around his West Yorkshire garden, where prairie planting is the order of the day, Ashley Edwards explores the resilience of drought-resistant plants at RHS Hyde Hall and Arit Anderson visits the garden of two designers who have blended three distinct planting styles into one

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: The Xudum prides mothers and young cubs flee south as four dangerous intruder male lions close in, threatening to kill cubs and take over the pride. For Pobe the cheetah, the pressure is on to provide for her family, but as one of the Okavangos underdogs, she must defend every meal from bigger cats. Leopard mum Lediba has no choice but to leave her cubs alone while she hunts, despite the sinister presence of an unknown male

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Having retired from boxing, former European cruiserweight champion Tommy McCarthy is taking his career from the ring to the stage as he launches a second career in stand-up comedy. With a wife and four daughters depending on him, can Tommy turn his part-time hobby as a comic into a full-time career?

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the days events

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: The End We Start From (2023)
programme.desc: A woman and her newborn must find their way home after an environmental crisis leaves London submerged by floodwaters, tearing the young family apart. Thriller, starring Jodie Comer, Joel Fry, Benedict Cumberbatch and Mark Strong

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Will Robots Take My Job? - Panorama
programme.desc: Reporter Richard Bilton investigates the rise of automation in the workforce, questioning if it will bring freedom from toil or leave millions discarded and impoverished. In Silicon Valley, plans are underway for massive changes in employment, prompting fears that millions of people will be replaced by robots and AI

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A cohort of junior doctors join the midwives on their district rounds, including Timothy Turner, while Violet turns her hand to midwifery

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from Scotland must go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again. They need to impress a panel of exacting judges - chef Tom Kerridge, former Great British Menu Champion of Champions Lorna McNee, and comedian Phil Wang. The guest judge is Bafta-winning actress Katherine Parkinson. How will she judge dishes celebrating the British film industry? Only one of the chefs will triumph and go through to represent Scotland at the national finals. Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: The Zero Line: Inside Russias War
programme.desc: Russian soldiers and civilians share their experiences of the war with Ukraine, including accounts of torture and summary executions to ensure obedience, and extreme brutality by the police to crush dissent on the home front

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Mysteries from Above
programme.desc: Nubian pyramids reveal the rise of female power in Ancient Kush. Plus, a shocking satellite image uncovers secrets of an ancient ancestral Puebloan site in New Mexico

programme.title: Evidence of the Unexplained
programme.desc: Thousands of UFO sightings are reported every year, along with terrifying abductions and other bizarre occurrences. This programme examines these accounts and explores recently declassified top secret UFO files, casting new light on historic cases

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Giorgio Tsoukalos reveals that in the nearly 2,000-year-old text called The Zohar there is a curious passage that describes seven Earths - each of which is said to be populated by both human and non-human intelligences

programme.title: Swamp People
programme.desc: The swamp heats up as Daniel and Big Tee face off against trespassers. Meanwhile, an old-timer teaches Troy some new tricks

programme.desc: As a heat wave grips the Everglades, Bruce and Zak call in canine reinforcements to sniff out serpents hiding from the rising temperature

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and dealers Tim Hogarth, Brenda Haller, James Layte and Tony Geering visit the Peak District town of Matlock to help members of the public sort the treasures from their trash. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five university friends join forces to take on the Tenable tower, answering questions about top 10 lists, then trying to score a perfect 10 in the final round. Sally Lindsay hosts

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers hosts as five dealers battle each other to buy items brought in by the public, including a 24ft-long table, a 1930s sign, a toy car, retro ad posters and a guillotine

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson, in the company of dealers Simon Schneider, Clive Attrell and Cheryl Hakeney, visits Evesham in Worcestershire to appraise items brought along by members of the public. Among other things, a carved walking stick makes a big impact at auction

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Coronation Streets Antony Cotton (Sean Tully) and actress Gemma Atkinson (Casualty, Hollyoaks) join forces with their relatives to guess the answers to surveys of 100 people in a bid to reach the final round, in which they can win up to £30,000 for a charity of their choice. Vernon Kay hosts

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: With the help of a celebrity panel, contestants must fill in the missing words of statements and phrases. Lily Savage hosts this classic game show.

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: With the help of a celebrity panel, contestants must fill in the missing words of statements and phrases. Lily Savage hosts this classic game show.

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Perry, Ros, Anna and Ben pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Another chance to see this Christmas special from 2005. Terry Wogan takes the hotseat alongside viewer Tim Radford, who successfully bid £40,000 to appear on the show in a Children in Need auction - fully aware that whatever money he and Terry win will also be going to the charity. The other pair hoping to win big are music manager and X Factor judge Louis Walsh and actress-singer Martine McCutcheon

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Family members Elisha, Emma, Georgia and Paul compete for cash by crossing a bridge of stepping stones across the studio floor. Once they have each faced the bridge on their own, those who have been successful join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the prize. Quiz show, hosted by Ross Kemp

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers hosts as five dealers battle each other to buy items brought in by the public, including a 24ft-long table, a 1930s sign, a toy car, retro ad posters and a guillotine

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis which sees a team of five friends from Southampton compete to answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.desc: Scott Mills, Gareth Malone, Louise Minchin and Glenn Hoddle must pit their wits against the Chaser, a ruthless quiz genius determined to stop them winning the cash prize. Hosted by Bradley Walsh

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: A classic episode of the award-winning quiz show presented by Chris Tarrant in which contestants can win up to one million pounds.

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts a special edition, inviting celebrities Robert Rinder, Andrea McLean, Brian McFadden and Denise Robertson to take on a Chaser in order to win thousands of pounds for the Text Santa TV appeal

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Peter, Kathryn, Zoe and Chris pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but if anyone is caught theyre out for good, leaving fewer players for the final chase

programme.title: Bullseye
programme.desc: Quiz that tests contestants skill at darts and their general knowledge

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Andrew is rushed to Queens Medical Centre after sustaining a severe facial injury from a machine at work. Trainee advanced clinical practitioner Emily Rowe oversees the 40-year-olds challenging treatment. In Resus, doctors need to work out the cause of 61-year-old Johns sudden blackouts, which are causing him to fall over, including falling down the stairs. Tony has come to AandE after being crushed by a forklift truck which trapped his ankle and cut his head

programme.title: Super Surgeons: A Chance at Life
programme.desc: Fifty-three-year-old ex-Naval Pilot Rich is suffering from a 16cm tumour that stretches from his jawline down to his heart, wrapping around his carotid artery. Consultant Head and Neck Surgeon Professor Vin Paleri requires close collaboration with vascular and cardiac specialists to delicately remove the tumour, potentially sacrificing the major artery in the process

programme.title: The Dunblane Tapes
programme.desc: The story of the grassroots campaign to ban private handguns in Britain through the recently uncovered recordings of a bereaved parent after the Dunblane school shooting in 1996. After the event, three mothers who were outraged that the perpetrator could legally carry four handguns and over 1,000 rounds of ammunition, launched a nationwide campaign to lobby the government to ban the private use of handguns in Britain, gaining support from the bereaved parents of Dunblane including John Crozier, who documented the most successful grassroots campaign in British history

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay steps in to assess a troubled Brooklyn restaurant thats sinking into debt. As an additional problem, its co-owners are constantly clashing. Can the acerbic chef help sort out the finances and operations before it folds?

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: American comedian and actress Desiree Burch joins Joe Lycett for some snow-filled shenanigans in the city of Rovaniemi - the official home of Santa Claus - in Finnish Lapland on the edge of the Arctic Circle. The pair try out ice floating by donning rubber suits and climbing into a freezing lake. After they thaw out, they head for a five-course tasting menu inspired by Lappish ingredients including reindeer. On day two, Joe and Desiree experience ice fishing, ride in a traditional Sami reindeer caravan, learn some local handicrafts and look for the Northern lights

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: Keith and Marj submit their application to transform the chapel into a home, pottery studio and events space. While they anxiously wait for the news, they turn their attentions to ripping out rot, learning some Welsh and opening the chapel to pitch their audacious plan to the local community

programme.title: Kirsties House of Craft
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp transforms fabric by dyeing it with turmeric, and embellishes an old lampshade

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer and Kirstie Allsopp catch up with Amy and Takie, who were desperate to buy their first property in London, but had to put their dream on hold until they could save enough to make buying worthwhile. There is also an update on Vicky and Andy, who combined their search for a home and two businesses in Wiltshire on a budget of £400,000 - an idea they eventually reconsidered when the reality of the market became clear

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with comedian Neil Delamere joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Woody has trouble proposing to Kelly, especially as Henri has already tricked her into saying she will marry him, claiming he is about to be deported. American bar-room comedy, starring Woody Harrelson and Jackie Swanson

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: When Rebecca gets the all-clear from the doctor she realises its Sam who needs to take a fertility test, while Frasier finds it difficult to make a will. Ted Danson stars, alongside Kirstie Alley

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam and Rebecca go on a baby-making weekend break but begin to have doubts about the whole thing when they realise they are not in love. Comedy, starring Ted Danson and Kirstie Alley

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Part one of three. Marie gives Debra the silent treatment for being rude to her, so Ray talks the pair into making amends - but as usual in the Barone household, his efforts backfire. Starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Part two of three. Marie and Debras feud continues, so Ray and Frank use it to their advantage, while Robert tries to get the warring pair to make friends. American comedy, starring Ray Romano, Patricia Heaton and Doris Roberts

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra accuses Ray of still not being able to read her moods, prompting them to reminisce fondly about the first night they spent together. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A message mistakenly left on an answering machine leads to a possible romance for Frasier, who rushes off to the airport to meet the caller. Guest starring Linda Hamilton, with Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A cancelled date leaves Niles, Martin and Daphne suspecting Frasiers supermodel escort is just a figment of his fevered imagination - so he tries to provide photographic evidence of the relationship. Comedy, with Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles and Frasier go gift-shopping with a vengeance as Martins 65th birthday approaches, vying with each other to be the apple of their fathers eye on his big day. Starring John Mahoney, Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Part one of two. Niles throws a fancy-dress Halloween party with a literary theme as a benefit for the Library Association, which he attends dressed as Cyrano de Bergerac, while a misunderstanding lets one or two skeletons out of the closet. Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce star

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike Wolfe tries to buy a set of art deco cigarette machines, and one of his and Frank Fritzs recent purchases leaves an appraiser speechless

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend head to Dorset to pick up a Ford Escort MK2 RS2000, which belongs to carpenter Mike. After his son was involved in a horrific road accident, Mike gave up work and supported him through a four-year recovery. Now, Mikes family want to give something back to offer thanks to such a devoted father by presenting him with his fully restored RS2000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Max McMurdo and Simon OBrien are in Woking, Surrey, where they find a sidecar - to be transformed into a boat - and a water cylinder that Simon might make into a trendy lamp

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with comedian Neil Delamere joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Crufts 2026
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman joins the team as Clare Balding presents live coverage of Crufts 2026 from the National Exhibition Centre. The action gets off to a flying start with all the thrills and speed of Flyball, as reporter Radzi Chinyanganya and commentators Jim Rosenthal and Graham Partridge call the live heats. Plus: Claudia Winkleman gives her first impressions of the worlds greatest dog show, and special guest Ellie Simmonds returns to her home patch in Birmingham for canine chat

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: In France, renovation plans give restaurateurs Andrew and Richard sleepless nights, while poor weather hits BandB hosts Janey and Sharon, who are hoping to launch a new cookery school in Corrèze. In Spain, things get too quiet at Abi and Darrens silent disco on the Costa Blanca, and Gordon and Shonas Marbella dream hangs in the balance

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The weeks final visit is to Helen and Carls self-catering glampsite, Peacock Lake, in Nottinghamshire. The hosts feedback has ruffled plenty of feathers throughout the week, so their guests expectations are sky-high, and lavatory letdowns and smelly bedding dont make for happy glampers

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The fourth to host in south Wales is childrens doctor Sophie, who despite usually being a vegan has decided to cook meat for her guests in an attempt to grab the grand. Sophies night is full of surprises as she reveals a number of hidden talents. Claire is flabbergasted at her job, Richard is shocked at where she went to university, and Sandra thinks she can give her a run for her money at her hidden hobby. The night isnt without incident as Sophies inexperience at cooking meat comes back to bite her and makes Daffyd uncomfortable

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander is in Lower Normandy in France with Tracy and Lee from Greater Manchester, who run a dog walking business together. The couple have a budget of £70,000 to buy a holiday property that could one day become their permanent home. Lucy hunts out five properties for the pair to consider

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Crufts 2026
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents all the live action as Crufts 2026 gets into full swing. And Claudia Winkleman hustles the bustle of the Birmingham NEC halls for the best features at the worlds greatest dog show. The Working Group is the first to be judged, with the winner going through to Sundays prestigious Best in Show final. This group includes guard dogs, and search and rescue breeds like Dobermanns, Rottweilers and Newfoundlands. Then its time for the Pastoral Group to take centre stage. Australian shepherds have been the most successful breed in this group in recent years. And if that wasnt enough excitement, athleticism and speed are the order of the day in the Agility and Flyball competitions, with commentary from Jim Rosenthal and Graham Partridge

programme.title: Molly vs the Machines
programme.desc: Documentary about the death of Molly Russell, the 14-year-old who in 2017 took her own life as a result of depression and negative social media content, and her father Ians fight for online safety. The film focuses on what happened to Molly in the lead-up to her tragic death and the broader economic logic behind AI and big tech as it continues to shape, influence and infiltrate peoples lives

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics views on Britains Got Talent, The Heat, 56 Days, Olivia Attwood: Getting Filthy Rich, Ed Staffords Rite Of Passage, Alice Roberts: Our Hospital Through Time, BBC News (Andrew Arrested) and ITV News (Mandelson Arrested)

programme.title: Lake District Rescue
programme.desc: The team attend a man who has slipped while crossing a stream and is left with a bone sticking out of his arm. In Wasdale, an emergency call has come in for a man with a badly broken leg on the Corridor Route to Scafell Pike. Darkness is closing in, and the first challenge is to locate the casualty. Leaving him on the hill in plummeting temperatures would be life-threatening

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to New Jersey to transform Maxs Bar and Grill. Even though its been serving up famous hotdogs for almost 100 years, its on the verge of crumbling, with over $1million of debt. The situation isnt helped by a power struggle between the divorced owners, who still work together

programme.title: French Exit (2020)
programme.desc: Frances Price, an ageing Manhattan socialite, cashes in the last of her possessions and resolves to live out her twilight days anonymously in a borrowed apartment in Paris, accompanied by her directionless son Malcolm and a cat named Small Frank. Comedy drama, starring Michelle Pfeiffer, Lucas Hedges and Valerie Mahaffey, with the voice of Tracy Letts

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien head to Dumfries to meet Maggie, whose farm holds an extraordinary collection of memorabilia

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: Now that permission has been granted, Keith and Marj start work in the main chapel hall, removing the pews, parquet floor and large pulpit. Keith visits a fellow potter, and Marj makes a discovery in the once-pigeon-infested Sunday school ceiling vents

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: Siobhán McSweeney hosts the semi-final, where the four remaining potters make wall-mounted water fountains inspired by Romes Trevi Fountain and take on guest judge and ceramic artist Cleo Mussis surprise mosaic challenge. Whose fountain will make a splash and see them through to the final, and whose mosaic will leave them picking up the pieces?

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with comedian Neil Delamere joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.sub-title: Dont Shoot, Im Only the Psychiatrist
programme.desc: Sam foolishly allows Woody to cut his hair, and Frasier brings the members of his therapy group for those with low self-esteem to the bar, in the hope they can meet normal people. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Woody has to take a second job as a gravedigger to pay for Kellys engagement ring and soon finds himself hallucinating due to lack of sleep. Starring Ted Danson and Woody Harrelson

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Carla opposes her sons decision to become a priest, Sam finds a way to get even with John Hill, and Cliff begins a new life as a stand-up comedian. Comedy, starring Ted Danson and Rhea Perlman

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Eccentric cousin Gerard persuades Robert to join a cult known as the Inner Path, which claims to teach its members how to attain peace and achieve their full potential. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Brad Garrett

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra learns two of her friends have been to see a marriage counsellor, and tries to convince Ray they should seek professional advice to resolve their relationship problems. Ray Romano stars

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Allys teacher overhears Ray commenting about the childrens excessive workload, she invites him to address the curriculum review board and gives him a mountain of material for review and evaluation. Ray Romano stars

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Part two of two. Roz panics at the thought of becoming a mother, prompting Frasier to lend her a friendly shoulder to cry on - while he ponders the fathers identity. Award-winning comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and Peri Gilpin

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Special episode filmed on location in Seattle. Frasier is guest of honour at a public rally to mark his 1,000th radio show - but the celebrations go awry as Niless jealousy reaches new peaks. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink is asked to be a guest speaker on an all-star cruise, and invites the family to accompany him. However, the so-called celebrities also appearing are not exactly famous. Meanwhile, Niless marital problems worsen once he discovers Maris will also be coming along

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The radio psychiatrist begins dating a successful lawyer, but it is not long before conflicting career demands start to get in the way of their relationship

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike Wolfe and Frank Fritz find a huge sculpture of Elvis and trawl through a trailer full of rare art and military memorabilia

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend head off to Loughborough, Leicestershire, to take on a 1992 Saab 900 Turbo thats in desperate need of repair. The car belongs to Saab enthusiast and former electrical engineer Pravesh. Sadly, due to ill health, Pravesh has been left with little strength to work on the car, so its been left to rot. His family believe getting his Saab back on the road would give him the boost he needs

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien visit Herefordshire to meet former farmer Julian. Henry finds an old cattle feeder that he wants to turn into a garden bench, while Simon hatches plans to turn a vintage seed drill into a trendy dining table

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with comedian Neil Delamere joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Crufts 2026
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman joins Clare Balding for more live coverage from the National Exhibition Centre, as dog owners from across the globe descend on Birmingham. This year, the Flyball is wide open with the reigning Belgian champions Road Runners Beep Beep not competing. Jim Rosenthal and Graham Partridge commentate on todays heats, while Radzi Chinyanganya captures all the emotion from the Main Arena. Plus: the latest from the NEC halls with Claudia Winkleman

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: The season winds down for the Brits abroad, but theyre gearing up for the biggest days of their lives. Its hoped that Abi and Darrens wedding in Spain and Andrew and Richards wedding event in France will go off without a hitch. Friends Janey and Sharon also have plenty to celebrate at their BandB - but success comes at a cost

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: With cleanliness being the buzz word of the week, almost all of the hosts are unhappy about their less-than sparkling scores and theres suspicions that some of the hoteliers have been playing dirty

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Civil servant Sandra hosts the final night in south Wales. After a week of culinary highs and lows, Sandras planning a menu thats heavy on cream and mushrooms - a risky move since her guests have made it very clear throughout the week that these two ingredients are not much-loved. With a new twist shaking things up during dessert, its a dramatic end to a lively week

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Winter Paralympics Live
programme.desc: Live coverage of the spectacular Opening Ceremony of the Milano Cortina 2026 Paralympic Winter Games from the Arena di Verona. This beautiful Roman amphitheatre is the first UNESCO World Heritage Site to be used in a Paralympic Ceremony. In the studio, Channel 4s presenting team of Ade Adepitan, Billy Monger, Ed Jackson, Dame Sarah Storey and Alice Tai preview the next nine days of elite sporting competition, highlighting the key athletes to watch out for and ParalympicsGBs main medal hopes. Among those athletes are curler Jo Butterfield, who is aiming to become the first British athlete to win at both the Summer and Winter Games, and Neil Simpson, wholl be wanting to defend his super-G title

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, with cameras capturing their instant reactions and the lively discussions that are prompted by the weeks biggest and best shows

programme.title: The Last Leg
programme.desc: Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker are joined by Nick Mohammed, Vittorio Angelone and Penny Mordaunt for a comic review of the significant moments of the past seven days. As usual, the hashtag #isitok paves the way for the gang to round up, examine and explain the leading and most entertaining news stories of the week

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert: The Book of John
programme.desc: A stand-up performance by the comedian filmed live at the Wales Millennium Centre in Cardiff, which was his first live show in six years. Raw, personal and brutally honest like never before, Rhod takes us on a no-nonsense journey into finding humour when youve hit rock bottom - and the unexpected encounter that leads you to a lower place

programme.title: Bill Bailey: Cosmic Jam
programme.desc: In his classic debut live stand-up show, filmed at Londons Bloomsbury Theatre in 1996, Bill explains why Bryan Adams is perhaps the greatest ever exponent of the rock anthem; demonstrates the influence that Cockney music has had on the great classical composers; and warns of the perils of a world ruled by insects, while playing the second-biggest gong in Britain

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Long Island, New York, to help an Indian restaurant which used to run a Mexican eatery out of the same kitchen. Its been operating for 35-years, but a decline in business led to fusion experiments, which havent always been successful and which have confused the customers. With some dishes tasting soggy and swimming with oil, Ramsay has his work cut out trying to turn things round

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Another selection of BandB owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. The contest begins at Knockaloe Beg Farm on the Isle of Man, where owners Fiona and John Anderson hope their guests will embrace their green ethos. They kick things off with an afternoon of laser mayhem, and continue the fun the next day by following breakfast with hands-on animal antics

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests head to the Arches on the Isle of Man for their second stay, where owner Phil Routledge hopes to wow them with scenic views and scuba diving at the BandBs own pool. However, some of the owners are dismayed by modern gadgets with a lack of instructions, and even find cobwebs in their rooms

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants arrive at the Trafford in Blackpool, owned by Robbie and John Welling, who are proud of their traditional bucket and spade BandB. The guests are pleasantly surprised, even if previous host Phil is intent on finding dust as a bit of tit-for-tat retaliation, and the afternoon is spent learning the art of making sticks of rock
